Title of the Journal with Published Year: IEEE: Personalized Career Recommendation System using Machine Learning Algorithms (2023)
Method: Uses standard Random Forest and Decision Tree classifier algorithms on static data.
Outcomes: Achieved standard baseline accuracy in predicting general IT roles for graduates.
Limitations: Pure classification algorithm; cannot dynamically generate detailed, personalized conversational text or roadmaps.

Method: Processed student datasets mapping academic skills to a static career matrix.
Outcomes: Successfully highlighted broad skill gaps for students based on historical data.
Limitations: Relied entirely on a fixed CSV dataset; lacked continuous integration with real-world scholarship trackers.

Title of the Journal with Published Year: International Journal of Advanced Research: Intelligent Career Recommendation System (2021)
Method: Used neural network models to correlate static user profiles with career paths.
Outcomes: Provided highly reliable, broad industry matches based on historical grading.
Limitations: Heavy dataset constraints with no natural language conversational interface for students.

Title of the Journal with Published Year: Journal of Educational Tech: A Web-Based Career Prediction Framework (2021)
Method: Constructed a digital questionnaire querying backend rule-based logic.
Outcomes: Transformed manual career counseling into a basic digital survey.
Limitations: Zero use of Large Language Models; rigid response structures that cannot adapt to highly unique student profiles.
